name: Scotland - Low income (£30,000)
description: Scottish taxpayer earning £30,000 - reaches the Intermediate rate band. Scotland's extra bands mean slightly different tax vs rest of UK at this level.

sources.description: Verified using Scottish income tax rates and official HMRC calculator
sources.notes: Scottish income tax validated using 6 progressive tax bands (19%, 20%, 21%, 42%, 45%, 48%). National Insurance uses UK-wide rates (same as rest of UK). Calculations verified arithmetically against official Scottish Government tax factsheet.

sources.description: Verified via official HMRC PAYE Tax Calculator and arithmetic validation using official 2026/27 rates
sources.notes: Income tax and National Insurance contributions verified against official HMRC calculator. NI calculation validated arithmetically using official rates: 8% on £12,570-£50,270, 2% above £50,270. Income tax validated using progressive brackets: 20%, 40%, 45% on taxable income after personal allowance deduction.
